uses: Malache rosa-campestris, also known as field mallow, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes. The plant's roots and leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory properties and are used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. In addition, field mallow is believed to have antimicrobial and antiseptic properties, making it useful for wound healing and treating infections. The plant is also used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. Furthermore, field mallow is thought to have a soothing effect on the digestive system, making it a potential remedy for stomach issues and indigestion.
